exercises.title: Trace Path operations
exercises.scenario: Path navigation methods — getParent, getFileName, getName.

exercises.title: Read all lines from a file
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank to read all lines from a file.
hints: Files.readAllLines(path) — reads entire file as List<String>

exercises.title: Write and read a file
exercises.instructions:
Write 'Hello from Hyderabad!' to 'greeting.txt', then read it back and print.

Expected output:
Hello from Hyderabad!
hints: Files.readString(path) reads all back as one String

exercises.title: Append to file with StandardOpenOption
exercises.scenario: APPEND option adds to existing content.
exercises.instructions:
Write three lines to a log file using APPEND (so all three lines accumulate). Print the final file content.

Expected output:
2024-01-01 INFO Started
2024-01-01 INFO Running
2024-01-01 INFO Stopped
exercises.starter_code:
import java.nio.file.*;
// Append three lines to a log file

test_cases.expected_stdout:
2024-01-01 INFO Started
2024-01-01 INFO Running
2024-01-01 INFO Stopped

exercises.title: Create directory structure
exercises.scenario: createDirectories makes the whole tree at once.
exercises.instructions:
Create directory tree `project/src/main/java` and `project/src/test/java`. Check they exist.

Expected output:
src/main/java exists: true
src/test/java exists: true
exercises.starter_code:
import java.nio.file.*;
// Create nested directory structure

test_cases.expected_stdout:
src/main/java exists: true
src/test/java exists: true

hints: No error if directory already exists

exercises.title: Count lines in a file with Files.lines
exercises.scenario: Files.lines returns lazy Stream<String> — memory efficient.
exercises.instructions:
Write a 10-line file, then count non-blank lines using Files.lines with try-with-resources.

Expected output:
Total lines: 10
Non-blank: 8
hints: Files.lines() returns Stream<String> — must close it!

exercises.title: Copy file to backup directory
exercises.instructions:
Create a source file, create a backup directory, copy the file. Copy again (should overwrite with REPLACE_EXISTING). Print sizes of both.

Expected output:
Source size: [N] bytes
Backup size: [N] bytes
Sizes match: true
hints: Files.copy(src, dest, REPLACE_EXISTING) — overwrites if exists
dest = backupDir.resolve(src.getFileName()) — put file in backup dir

exercises.title: Walk directory for CSV files
exercises.instructions:
Create a nested directory structure with CSV and non-CSV files. Use Files.walk to find and count only CSV files.

Expected output:
Found CSVs:
[paths of CSV files, sorted]
Total CSVs: 3

exercises.title: Process CSV with Files.lines stream
exercises.scenario: Stream file lines, skip header, parse fields.
exercises.instructions:
Read a product CSV (header + 5 data rows). Skip header, parse each row, print products costing > ₹1000 sorted by price desc.

CSV: name,category,price

Expected output:
Keyboard: ₹1999
Headphones: ₹2999
Monitor: ₹18999

exercises.title: File metadata and existence checks
exercises.scenario: Files utility methods for file inspection.
exercises.instructions:
Create a file and directory. Print: exists, isFile, isDirectory, size (bytes), last modified for each.

Expected output:
file.txt: exists=true, isFile=true, isDir=false, size=[N]
dataDir: exists=true, isFile=false, isDir=true
hints: 'sample data for size check' is 26 bytes

exercises.title: Move files after processing (archive pattern)
exercises.scenario: Process input files and archive processed ones.
exercises.instructions:
Create 3 text files in `input/`. Process each (count words), write summary. Move processed files to `done/`. Print summary.

Expected output:
Processed: file1.txt (3 words)
Processed: file2.txt (4 words)
Processed: file3.txt (5 words)
Moved 3 files to done/

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Processed: file1.txt (3 words)
Processed: file2.txt (4 words)
Processed: file3.txt (5 words)
Moved 3 files to done/

hints: Collect list first (can't move while iterating the stream)
Files.readString → split on whitespace → count words

exercises.title: Path resolution and normalization
exercises.scenario: resolve() joins paths; normalize() removes . and ..

exercises.title: Word frequency counter from file
exercises.scenario: Stream file lines, tokenize, count word frequencies.
exercises.instructions:
Read a text file with Files.lines. Count word frequencies (case-insensitive). Print top 5 by frequency descending.

Sample text: 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog the fox and the dog'

Expected output:
the: 4
fox: 2
dog: 2
brown: 1
quick: 1
hints: Sort by value desc, then key asc for ties

exercises.title: Directory diff — find files in A but not B
exercises.scenario: Walk two directories, compare file names.
exercises.instructions:
Given two directories, find files present in dir1 but not in dir2 (by filename). Print them sorted.

dir1: a.txt, b.txt, c.txt
dir2: b.txt, c.txt, d.txt

Expected output:
Only in dir1:
a.txt
Only in dir2:
d.txt

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Only in dir1:
a.txt
Only in dir2:
d.txt

hints: Collect filenames to Set<String> for each directory

exercises.title: Order CSV importer — full pipeline
exercises.scenario: Real file I/O pipeline: read, validate, transform, write results.
exercises.instructions:
Read an orders CSV (orderId, product, qty, unitPrice). Skip header. Validate each row (qty > 0, price > 0). Compute order total (qty × price). Write valid orders to `output/valid.csv` and invalid to `output/errors.txt`. Print summary.

CSV has 6 rows — 4 valid, 2 invalid (qty=0 and negative price).

Expected output:
Valid orders: 4
Invalid rows: 2
Total revenue: ₹[sum]
Output written to output/
hints: readAllLines: List; iterate with index (skip i=0 header)
Write header + valid rows to valid.csv; errors to errors.txt
